title: DORA Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) Lifecycle

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.26 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.26 (Threat-led penetration testing) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.17: DORA Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) Lifecycle.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.17' running on index itsm and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `dora_tlpt_register.csv` (TLPT engagement tracking), `index=itsm` (remediation tickets), ES Notable events (from purple team exercises)
spl:
| inputlookup dora_tlpt_register.csv
| eval last_tlpt_date=strptime(completion_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval months_since_tlpt=round((now()-last_tlpt_date)/(86400*30), 0)
| eval next_due=if(months_since_tlpt >= 36, "OVERDUE", if(months_since_tlpt >= 30, "DUE_WITHIN_6_MONTHS", "ON_TRACK"))
| eval findings_remediated=if(isnotnull(total_findings) AND isnotnull(findings_closed), round(100*findings_closed/total_findings,1), 0)
| table scope, tester_organization, completion_date, months_since_tlpt, next_due, total_findings, findings_closed, findings_remediated, critical_findings_open
| sort next_due
description: Article 26 requires financial entities identified by competent authorities to conduct TLPT at least every three years, following TIBER-EU methodology with qualified external testers. This use case tracks the TLPT lifecycle — from threat intelligence scoping through red team execution to remediation of findings — ensuring the advanced testing programme that DORA mandates for systemically important entities is completed and acted upon.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Create `dora_tlpt_register.csv` with TLPT engagement records (scope, tester, completion date, finding counts); (2) track three-year cycle per Art. 26; (3) monitor finding remediation — critical findings should be remediated within 90 days; (4) validate tester qualifications per Art. 27; (5) coordinate pooled TLPT with entities sharing the same ICT provider where applicable; (6) report TLPT status to competent authority. Indexes required: `index=itsm`.
visualization: Table (TLPT status), Single value (months since last TLPT), Gauge (finding remediation %), Bar chart (open findings by severity). Key fields: `completion_date`, `critical_findings_open`, `findings_closed`, `findings_remediated`, `last_tlpt_date`, `months_since_tlpt`.
knownFalsePositives:
1. **TLPT scoping phase extensions** — The threat intelligence phase may require longer than planned due to evolving threat landscape; scope lock dates adjust accordingly with management approval.

2. **Tester re-engagement for scope changes** — Material changes to the ICT landscape during TLPT execution may require scope adjustment with documented rationale.

3. **Competent authority feedback cycles** — Supervisory authority review of TLPT plans may require iterations that extend the planning timeline legitimately.

4. **Purple team vs. pure red team distinction** — Organisations transitioning from purple team exercises to full TLPT may show hybrid approaches during the maturity journey.

5. **Critical production freeze periods** — TLPT activities paused during business-critical periods (year-end processing) with documented suspension and resumption plan.

**Operational suppression:** Maintain a lookup table (`dora_approved_exceptions.csv`) mapping known legitimate activities by process owner, approval reference, and expiry date. Filter the SPL results against this lookup to suppress documented exceptions and reduce alert fatigue. Review and rotate entries quarterly per the ICT risk management framework review cycle.
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Lookups**: `dora_tlpt_register.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('dora_tlpt_register.csv')`.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.26`. The control objective is: "Evidence that DORA Art.26 (Threat-led penetration testing) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.17: DORA Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) Lifecycle." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is bespoke for this UC — no single canonical TA parses every source the detection relies on. Route data into the relevant indexes under the relevant sourcetypes, and document the ingestion path in the UC runbook so future engineers know where each field originates.


6 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** Create `dora_tlpt_register.csv` with TLPT engagement records (scope, tester, completion date, finding counts). The register referenced here is primary evidence — treat changes to it with the same discipline as code changes.

• **Hint 2:** track three-year cycle per Art. 26. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 3:** monitor finding remediation — critical findings should be remediated within 90 days. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 4:** validate tester qualifications per Art. 27. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 5:** coordinate pooled TLPT with entities sharing the same ICT provider where applicable. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 6:** report TLPT status to competent authority. Report-generation cadence must match the regulatory reporting cycle; a monthly digest satisfies an annual review requirement but not a quarterly one.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "DORA Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) Lifecycle," the aggregation produces the result shape required by the DORA control — each row is a summary record the regulation expects to be presentable at audit time.

This search begins with `| inputlookup dora_tlpt_register.csv` — the base dataset is a CSV/KV Store lookup, not live event data. This inverts the usual Splunk assumption: the detection does not stream from an index, it iterates a register. The register itself is the authoritative source of truth (contracts, policies, sign-offs, attestations) and the search joins it against indexed events to find gaps.

Lookup authority: treat `dora_tlpt_register.csv` as a primary compliance artifact. Its contents ARE the answer the regulator will ask for ('show me your vendor list,' 'show me your CDE inventory,' 'show me your policy exceptions'). Implement the following controls on the lookup itself, not just the search:

• Version the CSV in a git repository with a mandatory pull-request review for every change.
• Maintain a signed-record trail: who changed what, when, and with what approval. A lookup with a silent edit history is an audit liability.
• Automate a nightly diff: `| inputlookup dora_tlpt_register.csv | eval hash=md5(...) | outputlookup append=true dora_tlpt_register_audit.csv` — this produces an immutable record of the register's state over time.
• Lock the lookup with role-based access: only the content owner has `list_inputs` + `write_file` on the path; everyone else is read-only.

The live-data side of the join (across the full search range) is the detection trigger — when the register and the telemetry disagree, that's a finding.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.3.17: DORA Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) Lifecycle]
cron_schedule = 0 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.3.17,reg=DORA
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=the_index sourcetype=the_sourcetype earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `completion_date`, `critical_findings_open`, `findings_closed`, `findings_remediated`, `last_tlpt_date`, `months_since_tlpt`, `next_due`, `scope`, `tester_organization`, `total_findings`. Run:

```spl
index=the_index sourcetype=the_sourcetype earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table completion_date, critical_findings_open, findings_closed, findings_remediated, last_tlpt_date, months_since_tlpt, next_due, scope
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(d) Threshold calibration:** Before enabling as a production alert, run the base search with the threshold removed and profile the distribution of `months_since_tlpt`:

```spl
<base search> earliest=-30d
| stats perc50(months_since_tlpt) as p50 perc90(months_since_tlpt) as p90 perc99(months_since_tlpt) as p99 max(months_since_tlpt) as hi
```

The current threshold `36` should sit between p90 and p99 for a balanced signal-to-noise ratio. If it's below p50, expect heavy alert volume; if above p99, expect under-detection.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.3.17: DORA evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Drill-through table (`TLPT status`):** Full-width bottom row. Enable row-level drilldown to a secondary dashboard that expands each violation with its raw events, timeline, and related context. This is the panel the auditor will actually read — optimise it for scannability (avoid huge wrap-around fields).

**Panel 2 — Single-value KPI (`months since last TLPT`):** Place at top-left. Configure `underLabel` with the regulation clause (e.g. 'DORA current violation count') and set a red colour range for any non-zero value. A zero reading is the steady state for a healthy control.

**Panel 3 — Gauge (`finding remediation %`):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 4 — Distribution (`open findings by severity`):** Shows the volumetric distribution across the primary dimension. Use `sort` so the worst offender is first — auditors focus on the tallest bar.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**Search returns no results when violations are expected:** Verify time-window alignment, index existence, and field extractions in that order. Most 'missing results' are simpler configuration issues before they're detection-logic issues.

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**DORA-specific failure modes:**

• Third-party ICT risk register (Art. 28): DORA requires a Register of Information listing every critical ICT third-party service provider. Detections touching supplier risk must reference this register; if a supplier appears in telemetry but not in the Register, that is a Register-completeness finding, not just a technical anomaly.

• Testing cadence (Art. 24-27): DORA's TLPT (Threat-Led Penetration Testing) is required every 3 years for significant institutions. Detections measuring resilience testing must differentiate TLPT (supervisor-led) from self-assessment — mixing them dilutes the evidence.

• Incident classification thresholds (Art. 18): DORA uses specific criteria for 'major' vs 'significant' incidents — client impact, duration, data volume. Detections must classify against these specific thresholds, which differ from GDPR breach criteria; a single event can be major-DORA but not a personal-data breach.

• Resilience testing record retention (Art. 27): results of digital operational resilience tests must be retained. Align log retention to match — typically 5+ years. Verify `frozenTimePeriodInSecs >= 157680000` on any index named by a DORA resilience-testing UC.
grandmaExplanation: We stay on top of threat-led penetration testing (tlpt) lifecycle — so we can show we govern technology and resilience risk the way financial rules that apply to us require.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Set a test TLPT milestone to 30 days overdue with no completion evidence; verify the lifecycle search identifies the overdue milestone and alerts the TLPT programme manager.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Record all test TLPT milestones completed within their planned dates; verify the lifecycle dashboard shows green status throughout.

evidence: TLPT lifecycle evidence showing threat intelligence phase, scope lock documents, test execution timeline, findings report, remediation plans, and competent authority notifications.
exclusions: TLPT-exempt entities per proportionality determination; scope changes during threat intelligence phase with documented rationale; critical production freeze periods with approved suspension.
